mj-text-bKWuFGBO.content:
Hi {{contact.first_name}},

December marks the moment when conventional wisdom gets expensive.

While most sellers are "waiting for spring" and investors are "staying cautious," the actual data shows something completely different: Q4 multifamily transactions surged 51% year-over-year, and December single-family sellers are closing deals faster with stronger terms than they've seen in three years.

Here's what the market isn't telling you: The seasonal slowdown is a myth that costs people money. December buyers aren't browsing—they're closing. December inventory is 60% lower than spring, which means serious sellers face almost zero competition. And the multifamily dealflow that was frozen for two years? It's back, with better risk-adjusted returns than the 2021-2022 frenzy ever offered.

Translation: The opportunity isn't coming in 2026. It's here in December 2025. And it disappears the moment spring inventory floods back in.

This month, we're breaking down exactly how to capitalize:

✅ Why December is actually a seller's opportunity window (not a dead zone)
✅ Where the 51% surge in multifamily transactions is happening—and why
✅ The exact market shift that unlocked dealflow after 24 months of paralysis

Let's position you ahead of the wave.

mj-text-bKWuFGBO-piT1bAyZ-XkxBz27A.content: → Why Serious Buyers + Low Inventory = Stronger Outcomes for Smart Sellers

mj-text-zUU7TNhr.content:
Here's what nobody tells you about December home sales:

The buyers still looking in December aren't tire-kickers. They're people with deadlines. Job relocations. Life changes. Money ready to deploy.

Everyone else already quit.

The math is simple:

  • Same number of serious buyers

  • 60% fewer competing listings

  • = Your listing gets 2.5x more attention

What actually happens when you list in December:

You don't wait 45 days for showings. You get offers in 10-14 days.

You don't negotiate with 12 people making lowball offers. You negotiate with 2-3 qualified buyers who need to close.

You don't wonder if spring will be better. You're already closed and deployed that capital while everyone else is still "waiting for the perfect time."

The play:

List now. Close in January. Redeploy capital in Q1 before spring inventory floods the market and drives negotiating power back down.

Or wait for spring. Compete with everyone. And watch buyer leverage increase with inventory.

Your call.

mj-text-iwAeSLUN.content: 🏠 Deal Flow Is Back: Where Investors Are Closing in Q4

mj-text-TwppHz2a.content: → Multifamily Surges 51% as Buyers and Sellers Finally Meet on Pricing

mj-text-C6AJmHxe.content:
For two years, the multifamily market was frozen.

Sellers wanted 2021 prices.

Buyers wanted 2023 valuations.

Nobody moved.

Q4 2025 changed everything. Transactions up 51% year-over-year.

What broke the stalemate:

Reality #1: Sellers Blinked First

Bridge loans from 2022-2023 hit maturity. Extensions got expensive. Sellers who "waited for the market to recover" realized the market already recovered—just at different valuations.

Cap rates that were 4.0% in 2021? Now 5.5-6.5%.

That's not a correction. That's the new baseline.

Reality #2: Buyers Got Confident

Rate volatility settled. Economic outlook cleared. Suddenly, underwriting a 7-10 year hold made sense again.

Institutional capital that sat on the sidelines for 24 months? Deployed.

Private investors who "waited for the bottom"? Realized the bottom was 6 months ago.

Reality #3: The Best Deals Aren't Listed

Here's what's actually moving:

Value-add properties with deferred maintenance (20-30% below replacement cost)

Secondary markets with population growth (not the cities everyone talks about)

Distressed notes from lenders who need them off the books

These aren't on LoopNet. They're in sponsor networks. Private dealflow. Relationships built over years, not found on Google.

The numbers that matter:

Cap rates: 5.5-6.5% (finally compensating for risk)

Debt coverage ratios: 1.25-1.35x (actual breathing room)

Rent growth projections: 2-3% (realistic, not fantasy)

Exit assumptions: Modeled at 7.0% caps (conservative, not hopeful)

Translation: Returns are real again. Not propped up by assumptions. Not dependent on market appreciation. Actual cash flow from operations.

mj-text-DKUspLNS.content: 💰December 2025 Interest Rate Snapshot

mj-text-dcG0v2OP.content:
Here’s where financing stands this month for both multifamily and single-family deals.

Residential rates sourced from Freddie Mac, NerdWallet, Bankrate, Yahoo Finance as of December 10, 2025. Commercial rates sourced from Commercial Loan Direct, Apartment Loan Store, Select Commercial as of December 8-10, 2025.

mj-text-Lvtk2ipD.content: 🔑 Key Takeaway: Market Wisdom for December 2025

mj-text-sr3BwGDZ.content:
If you're selling single-family:

December is not slow. December is strategic.

Fewer competitors. Serious buyers. Faster closes.

List now. Close Q1. Redeploy before everyone else wakes up.

If you're investing in multifamily:

The "wait and see" phase is over.

Deal flow is back. Operators are deploying. Terms are better than they've been in three years.

The question isn't "should I invest?" It's "who am I investing with?"

Because here's the truth: bad operators will lose money in good markets AND bad markets.

Good operators make money in any market.

Your job? Find the good operators. Before everyone else does.

mj-text-eY3Yqusd.content: Ready to Stop Waiting and Start Building?

mj-text-3rQjpfCg.content:
Markets don't wait for permission.

Spring 2026 won't magically have better deals. It'll have more competition.

Q1 won't suddenly have lower rates. It'll have more buyers fighting for the same properties.

The opportunity is now. December 2025.

Most people will realize this in March 2026.

You're reading this in December.

That's your edge.

What to do next:

Selling a property? Start the conversation now. Q1 closings start with December decisions.

Deploying capital? Review dealflow before everyone else floods back in.

Building a portfolio? The operators moving now are the ones who'll have the best 2026 performance.

Whether you're deploying $50K in your first syndication or managing a $100M family office portfolio, our mission remains the same:

✅ Identify distressed opportunities before they hit the market
✅ Structure favorable acquisitions with optimal debt placement
✅ Execute value-add strategies that create forced appreciation
✅ Time exits for maximum returns when the cycle turns

The opportunities are here. The capital is available. The question is: Will you be positioned to act?

Brian Norton
Principal, Olive Tree Investments
